data.title: UK police charge two people with running cryptocurrency scam
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News British police arrested and charged two men for suspected cryptocurrency fraud, causing a 75-year-old Aberdeenshire retiree to lose six figures.
The male victim reported the fraud in July 2024, and the Scottish police immediately launched an investigation. Officers from the Northeast Division of the police went to England yesterday to make arrests and cooperated with the West Midlands and South Yorkshire police. They arrested a 54-year-old man in Coventry and a 36-year-old man in Mixborough, both of whom were charged on the same day.
In a statement about the arrest, Detective Sergeant David Williamson of the Northeast Division Cyber ​​Crime Team warned the public to "be wary of activities that appear to be fraudulent and report them to the police." (Decrypt)

data.title: Trump considers raising lumber tariffs to 25%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to market news, Trump is considering raising lumber tariffs to 25%.

data.title: DeFi Festival Anticipated To Spark Prolonged Growth In Cryptocurrency Sector
data.description:
According to Odaily, Charles d’Haussy, CEO of the dYdX Foundation, shared insights during an interview at the Consensus 2025 conference in Hong Kong. He suggested that the cryptocurrency industry might experience a "DeFi Festival" as early as September, potentially leading to a prolonged surge in decentralized finance (DeFi) activities. 

D’Haussy expressed that the term "DeFi Summer" is insufficient to describe the anticipated upward trend, proposing "DeFi Festival" as a more fitting description due to its expected extended growth. He remarked, "In people's minds, DeFi Summer was like a three-month wild party. I believe that brief period is over. What follows will be a long-lasting party spanning several months."

Data from Steno Research indicates that the DeFi Summer began in 2020, marked by a significant increase in market adoption and a total value locked (TVL) surge to $15 billion. However, this momentum cooled during the bear market of 2022.

data.title: Yesterday, the U.S. Bitcoin spot ETF had a net outflow of $64.1 million, and BlackRock IBIT had no inflow/outflow
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Farside Investors data, the U.S. Bitcoin spot ETF had a net outflow of $64.1 million yesterday, with net outflows for two consecutive days. Among them, BlackRock IBIT had no inflows/outflows, and the trading volume reached $1.6 billion.

data.title: Aptos Research Director Criticizes Monad's Alleged Technology Copying
data.description:
According to Odaily, Aptos Research Director Alexander Spiegelman recently expressed confusion on X about why Monad is allegedly spending significant time replicating Aptos's technology. Spiegelman noted that Aptos's technology is open-source and supported by numerous peer-reviewed papers, suggesting that Monad should openly adopt it instead of concealing their efforts.

In response, Monad Co-Founder James Hunsaker stated, "I was studying software transactional memory (STM) in a Haskell environment long before you were out of diapers. BlockSTM is a simple extension of these concepts. I have never looked at any Aptos code, and frankly, I wouldn't have thought of Aptos if you hadn't posted this nonsense."

data.title: dYdX Foundation CEO: The industry may usher in the "DeFi Festival" as early as September, and the popularity will be greater than before
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News dYdX Foundation CEO Charles d’Haussy said in an interview at the Consensus 2025 Conference in Hong Kong that the cryptocurrency industry may usher in a "DeFi Festival" as early as September, triggering a DeFi craze that will last for "several months." He said that the term "DeFi summer" is not enough to describe the upward trend he believes is coming, and believes that "DeFi festival" is a more accurate term because it will continue to grow. "In people's minds, DeFi summer is like a three-month crazy party. I think this short period is over. After that, it will be a long party that will last for several months." According to Steno Research, DeFi summer began in 2020, when market adoption surged and TVL soared to US$15 billion, and then cooled down when the bear market hit in 2022. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Despite 40% drop in fee income, pump.fun still earned $247 million in less than 2 months
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, pump.fun transferred 65,000 SOL (US$11.01 million) to Kraken 5 hours ago.
Due to the diversion of BSC funds/attention and the influence of the "conspiracy group" of LIBRA's coin issuance, people's PvP transactions on pump have decreased, so the handling fees earned by pump are about 40% less than before, so the number of coins sold this time is also less.
However, less than 2 months have passed in 2025, and pump has sold 1.235 million SOL (US$247.45 million). Of course, another way to say it is: in less than two months, pump has earned US$247 million.

data.title: Nic Carter: The era of Memecoin as a fair trading opportunity is over, regulators may take action against insider trading
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Castle Island Ventures partner Nic Carter said that the era of meme coins as a fair trading opportunity is "undoubtedly over."
Carter believes that meme coins (tokens with little use other than speculative trading) were initially attractive because they seemed to provide a level playing field for retail investors. However, with the recent scandals such as the LIBRA coin, the market has been taken over by insiders, pre-launch trading, and robot-driven exchanges, putting everyday traders at a disadvantage.
He wrote: "The whole premise of meme coins is that they provide a 'fair launch' opportunity, and retail investors have the same opportunities as funds and VCs. This has been exposed as a lie-the casino is not fair."
While Carter believes that the recent trading frenzy has ended since US President Trump launched TRUMP, he did point out that the industry will not disappear. On the contrary, there may still be some new token issuances and some winners, but "the meta era is over."
As confidence in Meme Coin fades, Carter expects regulators to take action against insider trading in the industry, "Just because Meme Coin may not be a security, it doesn't mean that insider trading is not liable," predicting that blockchain transaction history will lead to future enforcement actions.
Looking forward, Carter believes the market will move toward more sustainable and fairer token issuance. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Aptos research director accused Monad of plagiarism, Monad co-founder denied it and said "I was developing it when you were still in diapers"
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Aptos Research Director Alexander Spiegelman wrote in X that he didn't understand why Monad spent so much time plagiarizing Aptos' technology. All of this is open source, and there are already many peer-reviewed papers. Instead of hiding it, it's better to plagiarize it directly.
In response, Monad co-founder James Hunsaker said: "When you were still wearing diapers, I was already studying software transactional memory (STM) in the Haskell environment. BlockSTM is a simple extension of these things. I have never seen any Aptos code. In fact, if you hadn't published this nonsense, I wouldn't have thought of Aptos."

data.title: SEC Withdraws Appeal Against Ruling on DeFi Regulation
data.description:
According to Odaily, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has voluntarily withdrawn its appeal against a ruling that prevented the agency from expanding existing securities laws to include decentralized finance (DeFi) users and projects. The SEC submitted a four-page motion to the U.S. Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals expressing its intention to withdraw the appeal, which faced no opposition.

In November of last year, a federal judge in Texas ruled that the SEC's attempt to broaden the legal definition of 'dealers' exceeded its authority. The judge found that the changes to the SEC's dealer rules were unlawful as they conflated DeFi traders with financial brokers.

Kristin Smith, CEO of the Blockchain Association, stated that the SEC's decision to withdraw the appeal marks a significant victory for the digital asset industry. She emphasized that the initial lawsuit against the SEC was filed to challenge the agency's unauthorized attempt to redefine its statutory powers. Smith expressed hope for productive dialogue between the industry and the SEC following the dismissal under the agency's new leadership.
